title: Herding runtime adapter: agent spawn command config-driven (herding.agent_command template), default byte-equivalent claude, codex example documented
action: Per D4: control-loop.sh (:284-289) and SKILL.md (:220, :262, :399) hardcode claude CLI invocations. Introduce a config seam: .bee/config.json optional herding.agent_command (working-agent spawn) and herding.control_command (control panes) as command templates with documented placeholders (e.g. {PROMPT}, {MODEL}); when absent, the built default is the EXACT current claude command — zero behavior change without config. control-loop.sh reads the config via node -e JSON read (mirroring how the script already sources repo config if it does; otherwise a small helper) with safe quoting. SKILL.md: replace the hardcoded spawn lines with the template mechanism, keep the claude default visible, and add a codex example block (codex exec form) explicitly marked as an adapter example, not a supported native herding mode. Do not touch enable/disable/merge gesture semantics (herding knowledge R2/R3/R11). Regen obligation inside THIS cell: node scripts/render_plugin_skill_trees.mjs && node skills/bee-hive/scripts/onboard_bee.mjs --repo-root . --apply && node scripts/release_manifest.mjs --write.

truths: with no herding config keys, spawned commands are byte-equivalent to today's
merge stays a single-shot owner gesture; dispatch interlock untouched
artifacts: SKILL.md documents the seam + codex example
prohibitions: no automation may call herding enable/disable/status verbs

trace.outcome: Herding spawn is now config-driven (D4): control-loop.sh reads optional .bee/config.json herding.control_command (JSON array of argv tokens, per-token {PROMPT}/{MODEL}/{MAX_TURNS}/{ALLOWED_TOOLS} substitution, never eval/join-then-split); absent -> byte-equivalent default. SKILL.md documents the same seam for herding.agent_command (the working-agent spawn tail in §8) plus a codex adapter example marked as illustrative-only. Enable/disable/status verbs and merge-gesture semantics untouched.
trace.verification_evidence:
{
  "tests_inspected": ["skills/bee-hive/templates/tests/test_herding.mjs"],
  "tests_added_or_changed": [],
  "red_failure_evidence": "Before this cell, control-loop.sh's run_iteration had no config seam at all: git show HEAD~1:skills/bee-herding/scripts/control-loop.sh (pre-change) shows the invocation hardcoded as `claude -p \"$PROMPT\" --model sonnet --max-turns \"$TURN_CEILING\" --allowedTools \"$ALLOWED_TOOLS\"` with no herding.control_command or herding.agent_command read anywhere in the repo (confirmed via rg for 'herding.control_command'/'herding.agent_command' returning zero hits pre-change). No automated test exercised a config-driven path because none existed; this is new surface, not a bug fix, per the deliberate_exceptions note below.",
  "verification_run": "bash -n skills/bee-herding/scripts/control-loop.sh -> OK; node scripts/run_verify.mjs --impacted-from-git --level 1 -> exit 0 (0 suites mapped from 28 changed files, full verify delegated to CI per ci-owned-verify D1/D6); node scripts/release_manifest.mjs --check -> exit 0, 510 files match. Existing regression suite skills/bee-hive/templates/tests/test_herding.mjs re-run after the change: 10 passed, 0 failed (no regression). Manual isolated verification in a scratch main-root (outside this repo's tracked tree, using a stubbed `claude`/replacement binary on PATH so no real claude session was ever invoked): (a) no herding.control_command key -> stub recorded the byte-equivalent default argv (claude -p <PROMPT> --model sonnet --max-turns 50 --allowedTools <TOOLS>); (b) herding.control_command set to a custom argv array -> the configured binary ran instead, with {MODEL}->sonnet, {MAX_TURNS}->50, {ALLOWED_TOOLS}->the enumerated allowlist, and the full multiline {PROMPT} all substituted into their own single argv elements intact; (c) a token containing a command-substitution attempt and an `rm -rf` fragment was passed through as inert literal text (confirmed no side-effect file was created) - proves the per-token substitution is not eval'd or re-split by the shell.",
  "deliberate_exceptions": ["No new automated regression test was added for the config seam itself (only manual scratch-directory verification, documented above) - this is new adapter surface behind an opt-in config key with a proven byte-equivalent default when absent, and the existing test_herding.mjs suite (10/10) proves no regression to the pre-change default path."]
}
trace.verify_output: bash -n: OK (no syntax errors). run_verify --impacted-from-git --level 1: 0 suites mapped from 28 changed files (all UNMAPPED -> full verify delegated to CI per D6/ci-owned-verify), exit 0. release_manifest --check: 510 file(s) match stored manifest, exit 0. Additionally, isolated manual tests confirmed: (1) existing skills/bee-hive/templates/tests/test_herding.mjs suite -> 10 passed, 0 failed (no regression); (2) with no herding.control_command config key, control-loop.sh real invocation is byte-equivalent to the pre-change hardcoded claude -p PROMPT --model sonnet --max-turns N --allowedTools TOOLS (verified via a stubbed claude binary capturing argv); (3) with herding.control_command set, the configured command runs instead (verified via stubbed replacement binary), with placeholders PROMPT/MODEL/MAX_TURNS/ALLOWED_TOOLS substituted per-token; (4) a token containing shell metacharacters (a command-substitution attempt plus rm -rf) was passed through as literal argv text and never executed or expanded, confirming the no-shell-injection requirement.
